observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management come up with estimated amount of expenses to be incurred upon titling of the lands particularly the old lands and include this amount in the budget of the Municipality. Prioritize titling of land with existing structures such as the municipal hall and grounds.

Recommendations:

We recommended that Management, through the Accounting Unit to: 

a. Fully adhere with Sections 1.1 (Granting of Cash Advances) and 1.2 (Liquidation of Cash Advances) of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

b.	Strengthen internal control in the granting of Cash Advances to avoid unliquidated Cash advances at year end which shall include but not limited to establishing cash advance cut-offs on December, serving of notices/demand letters, monitoring those with previously unsettled balances and prohibiting them from drawing new cash advances unless prior CAs are already settled; 

c.	Prohibit settlement of cash advance on staggard basis, immediate liquidation should be applied whereof;  

d.	Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers and schedules with complete details particularly on the purpose and dates covered of the travel per employee to accurately monitor cash advance balances; and

e. Instruct the concerned officials/employees to immediately liquidate their cash advances.

Recommendations:

We recommended that Management:

a.	Conduct an inventory of drugs and medicines and recognize these assets in the books of the LGU. For donated drugs and medicines as well as unrecorded medical supplies, refer to Property Transfer Report and other equivalent documents in recognizing the cost of these assets. In the absence of indicated cost in the said reports, coordinate with the source agencies. 

b.	Use the asset method in recording purchased medicines. To recognize distributed drugs and medicines, the Rural Health Unit should submit a month-end report indicating the quantity and cost of distributed medicines; and

c.	Direct the Rural Health Unit to assign a personnel in-charge of inventory management including but not limited to maintaining an active records of drugs and medicines received, distributed and balance on-hand including the expiration dates of such, conduct random inventory counting of drugs and medicines and reconcile with records. Immediately distribute medicines that are nearly expiring and immediately dispose expired medicines.
observations.managementAction: Received donated drugs were already recorded. However, no inventory of drugs and medicines was yet conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management through the Human Resource Department and Accounting Office:

To see to it that monthly net take-home pay of employees shall not be less than ₱5,000. In the event that total authorized deductions shall reduce net take home pay to ₱5,000, deductions shall be made in accordance with the order of preference under Section 55 of the General Provisions of the 2022 GAA

b. Through the HRMO to consider implementing financial wellness programs to assist them. These programs could include financial counseling or education, debt management advice, or even assistance with consolidating or refinancing loans, or provision of resources and information about financial planning and budgeting;

c.Through the HRMO to also consider negotiating with the financial institutions that provide the loans to their employees. Management  can try to secure more favorable loan terms, such as lower interest rates or longer repayment periods, which would result in lower loan payments and higher take-home pay for the employees.
observations.reason:
There are still many LGU employees whose monthly net take home pay are below the mandated ₱5,000.




Accounting office already informed employees that new loans will not be granted if net pay falls beyond the ₱5,000 minimum requirement.




Discussed during the exit conference that the Municipal Accountant already requested the institutions to cancel the existing agreements and to created new agreement for new loan accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:
As to obsolete checks, prepare a letter-request for disposal/destruction of these accountable forms accompanied by the inventory of checks for disposal which will be witnessed by COA. Pending final disposal, include these obsolete checks in the CRAAF.

2.12   We recommended and the Management agreed, through the Accounting Unit, to:
Strengthen internal control in the granting of cash advances which includes requiring the concerned officials and employees to fully liquidate their cash advances before any new cash advance will be processed so as not to accumulate the unliquidated balances;
Immediately inform and/or send demand letters to the concerned officials and employees as soon as their cash advances falls due; and
Prohibit settlement of cash advance on a staggard basis and immediately require refund of any unspent amount of the cash advance.

3.7   We recommended and the management agreed to review the validity of these recorded payables. If found valid, inform in writing the concerned suppliers and contractors about their unclaimed checks and final billings with the advice that the amount will be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality, in the absence of a proper claim made within a reasonable period. If found invalid, automatically revert the recorded accounts payable to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality.

5.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to prioritize the reconciliation of the remittance files with the billing files to address the outstanding discrepancies. Re-evaluate the tasks of LGU personnel particularly the job order workers and assign personnel who will concentrate on document retrieval and updating of service records. Closely coordinate with the GSIS and submit the necessary service records and remittance files to substantiate the remittances made.

10.10    We recommended and the Management agreed to discontinue the practice of requiring department heads to sign job-order contracts. Instead, require the Local Chief Executive to approve the job-order contracts himself. Ensure that these contracts are already available and properly signed prior to the disbursement of the first salary of the job order workers.

11.13    We recommended and the Management agreed to:
11.13.1 Require the LDRRMO to include in the LDRRMF Investment Plan all the funds available for appropriation to ensure that the utilization of the fund is supported and incorporated in the investment plan and to maximize the LGU’s institutional capacity for disaster risk reduction and management; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to timely furnish the LDRRM Officer of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF which is to be furnished to the Office of Civil Defense through the Department of Interior and Local Government and to the Commission on Audit.

13.9  We recommended and the Management concurred to provide legal basis as to the grant of the additional allowance to the employee. In the absence of the legal basis, we recommend the management to immediately discontinue the practice of granting additional allowance to an employee.

14.12 We recommended and the Management agreed, through the Bids and Awards Committee, to institute an internal control mechanism that would monitor the timely submission of perfected purchase orders and contracts to the audit team.
14.13 Instruct the Chair of the BAC, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the General Services Officer to submit immediately all the remaining unsubmitted contracts and Purchase Orders for the procurement of goods, with complete supporting documents enumerated in Paragraph 3.1.2 and the checklist in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2009-01.

We recommended and the Management is amenable to:
Immediately discontinue the practice of splitting requisitions and purchase orders so as not to circumvent the law on procurement. Competitive bidding shall be adopted in all its procurement activities and resort to alternative methods of procurement only if the conditions warrant; and
In preparing purchase requests, specifications shall be based on relevant characteristics, functionality and/or performance requirements. No brand names shall be specified in the purchase request.

We recommended that Management:  

a. Fully adhere with P.D. No. 1445 and Section 1.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001; and 
 
b. Refrain from the current practice of detaching documents from the Check Disbursement Voucher. Instead, necessary supporting documents such as computation of net pay, program of works and activity design must be provided in the disbursement vouchers for the grant of cash advances.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officials: 

a. To see to it that the excessive cost/negative variance will be deducted from the succeeding claims or any amount due to contractors and/or deduct it from the retention money or other securities posted by contractors, whichever is applicable and submit proof of deductions in compliance with the technical results; 

b.	Submit the required documents and/or explanation/justifications, if any, to facilitate re-evaluation of the technical aspects of the contracts and

c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DPWH Department Orders in the preparation of the ABC, POW, Plans and Drawings t o avoid errors in the quantities, measurements and descriptions. Otherwise, the same shall be disallowed in audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management create an inventory committee to con-duct a physical inventory on the municipality’s proper-ties in compliance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS.

We also recommended creating an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to appraise the value of said properties and prepare a report thereon. Of which, the report should be classified for each type of Property, Plant and Equipment for purposes of comparison / re-conciliation with the general ledger balances; and that the condition of the properties should be indicated to deter-mine those unserviceable / obsolete and assets still serviceable but no longer being used.

Furthermore, we recommended requiring the same Committee to work on the disposal of the unserviceable properties to be dropped from the books.

Likewise, we recommended instructing the Accounting Department to maintain Property Ledger Cards of all Property, Plant and Equipment owned by the Municipality and must be kept updated to facilitate reconciliation of ac-counting records with the Inventory Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed directing the Municipal Accountant to take the following courses of action:
Prepare a compound journal entry to adjust the identified book reconciling items pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 2.2 and 2.3 of COA Circular No. 96–002 dated October 2, 1996 by effecting the following entry:
Cash in Bank–LCCA		P48,520(See Annex B–2)
Prior Period Adjustments			         P48,520
#
Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and the authorized government depository bank to identify the benefactors that made direct deposits and payees that made direct deductions to the Municipality’s account;
Prepare a compound journal entry to close the prior year bank reconciling items by effecting the following entry:
Prior Period Adjustments		P7,070
Cash in Bank–LCCA 			P7,070
#
Trace the payees attributable to the above–mentioned stale checks and revert back to the CIB–LCCA and the appropriate liability account as of the period to conform with sound internal control as provided by Section 59 Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended and the Management agreed directing the Municipal Accountant to take the following courses of action:
Strictly adhere to PPSAS 17–Property, Plant, and Equipment and the PPSAS Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs on the recognition of depreciation on PPEs and not to confuse depreciation as impairment;
Prepare a lapsing schedule for accurate computation of the depreciation on all the Municipality’s depreciable assets; and
Effect the necessary correcting entries to derecognize “Accumulated Impairment Loss” and recognize depreciation for the current and prior years:
To derecognize the recorded Accumulated Impairment Loss:
Accumulated Impairment Loss–Name of PPE 	x x x
Government Equity					x x x
#
To recognize depreciation for the current year:
Depreciation Expense				x x x
Accumulated Depreciation				x x x
#
To recognize depreciation for the prior years:
Prior Period Adjustments				x x x
Accumulated Depreciation				x x x
#

We recommended and the Management committed to discontinue charging expenditures out of its 20% Development Fund which are not in accordance with Sections 3.0 and 4.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2017–01 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended and the Management agreed directing its Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report duly approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity to the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) days at the end of the semester to avoid sanctions of noncompliance and likewise instruct the same to furnish a copy of the PMRs duly acknowledged by the GPPB to the Office of the Auditor for review and evaluation.

We recommended and the Management agreed directing the Accounting Office to strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2015–009 dated December 1, 2015 and the PPSAS Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs and debit the account “Transfers of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds” for succeeding transfers of unspent DRRM.

We recommended and the Management committed that:
In addition to the reportorial requirements of the DILG regarding utilization of DRRMF, direct the LDRRMO to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in its prescribed format as duly certified by the Municipal Accountant in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012–002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month; and
Should there be DRRM funds sourced from GAA and cash donations, instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Receipt and Utilization of DRRMF sourced from GAA and Report on Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2014–002 dated April 15, 2014 and submit the same to the NDRRMC through the Office of Civil Defense within five (5) days at the end of each month, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the deadline on the submission of all paid vouchers, payrolls and other financial reports pursuant to Section 6.0 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1998 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009–006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Instruct the incumbent BAC Chairman to submit the perfected contracts and its supporting documents within the reglementary period of five days in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009;
Direct the same to comply the lacking documentary requirements to facilitate the technical and auditorial reviews of the COA–Technical Services Office and the COA auditors, respectively; and
Direct the same to coordinate with the former BAC Chairman and members to hasten the consolidation of pertinent documents relating to the above–mentioned projects to avoid suspensions and disallowances.
Management Comment:
During the exit conference, the Management asked for consideration regarding this matter and committed to submit the lacking documents as soon as possible.

We recommended and the Management agreed directing the Supply Officer concerned to strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 77–61 regarding fuel consumption and instructing the same to submit the driver’s trip tickets to the Office of the Auditor and likewise prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels along with the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption within 10 days of the succeeding month as further prescribed by Section 361 of the GAAM for proper monitoring and control of fuel–related expenditures.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the municipal officials concerned to strictly adhere to sound internal control as prescribed by Section 2.2 of COA Circular No 97–002, Sections 10 and 48 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 and Items II–A and II–B of BIRRevenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 and encourage the same to establish a credit line system to one of the gasoline stations in the locality or in its neighboring municipalities if awarded the contract and discontinue the practice of procuring fuel through reimbursement basis.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:
Direct its Municipal Accountant Designate to comply with the prescribed documentary requirements and see to it that complete supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers; and
Advise the same to go over the provisions of COA Circular 2012–001 and instruct him not to accept and process disbursement vouchers without the necessary supporting documents as required by said Circular.

We hereby recommended that Management effect an adjustment on the overstatement of RATA through reimbursement of the amount overpaid and that for the ensuing months, RATA shall be pro-rated based on the actual work/attendance of the official in accordance with the abovementioned Circular.
We also recommended that payment for RATA should be paid separate from the monthly salary; and a Certification by the Secretary of the Sanggunian for their attendance to session and another Certification by the Human Resource Management Office for No Vehicle Assigned by the Government should be attached.

We recommended that Management, starting January 1, 2017 (effectivity of GAA 2017) strictly comply with the provisions under Section 47 of RA 10924 otherwise known as The General Appropriations Act of 2017 which provides that in no case shall deductions be made to reduce the employee’s monthly net take home pay to an amount lower than Four Thousand Pesos (P4,000.00).

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 95–006, COA Circular No. 2009–006, Section 70 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, and COA Circular No. 96–011 in the submission of Disbursement vouchers, Official Receipts, and Bank Reconciliation Statements to provide timely post–audit of transactions during the year; and
Require the same to submit the reports, documents and records for the month of December 2016 to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly adhere to the rules and regulations set forth by COA Circular Nos. 2012–001 and 97–002 to avoid sanctions of noncompliance; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant–Designate to withhold the salary of the Accountable Officer/s concerned until the full liquidation of the said cash advances

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions set forth in RA No. 7160 and DBM Local Budget Circular No. 98 observing the 55% PS limitation and provide an explanation and / or justification on why the actual personal services incurred exceeded the 55% PS limitation.
We also recommended that Management establish a plausible scheme on generating more revenues and / or reduce its PS expenses.
Lastly, we recommended refraining from granting additional benefits and / or extra cash gifts in excess of the PS limitation to avoid disallowances.

We recommended that Management create an inventory committee to conduct a physical inventory on the municipality’s properties in compliance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS.
We also recommended creating an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to appraise the value of said properties and prepare a report thereon. Of which, the report should be classified for each type of Property, Plant and Equipment for purposes of comparison / reconciliation with the general ledger balances; and that the condition of the properties should be indicated to determine those unserviceable/obsolete and assets still serviceable but no longer being used.
Furthermore, we recommended requiring the same Appraisal and Disposal Committee to work on the disposal of the unserviceable properties to be dropped from the books.
Likewise, we recommended instructing the Accounting Department to maintain Property Ledger Cards of all Property, Plant and Equipment owned by the Municipality and must be kept updated to facilitate reconciliation of accounting records with the Inventory Report.
We recommended that Management prepare and submit the monthly reports as required by the COA Circulars 2012–002 and 2014–002 regarding utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipality discontinue the practice of charging to 20% Development Fund programs, projects and activities that are not in accordance with the policies and guidelines governing the utilization of such fund.
Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive should ensure the strict utilization of the fund in accordance with the issued guidelines to avoid future disallowances in audit.

We recommended that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report and submit the same to the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format in accordance with the specified time frame stipulated by the law and also furnish a copy to the Office of the Auditor.

We have recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to fill in the necessary information in the Purchase Orders (POs) regardless of amount as it provides clearcut terms and conditions in fulfilling the respective obligations of the Procuring Entity and the supplier as per COA Circular 96–010.
We also recommended directing the BAC to see to it that the information contained in the Purchase Orders are consistent with the information in other pertinent counterpart documents in order to avoid confusion.

We recommen-ded Management to demand imme-diate liquidation / return of the Cash Advances from the accountable officers, other-wise, charge them against their pay-roll.

We also recom-mend strict com-pliance of the guidelines for granting of Cash advances to pre-vent outstanding cash advances be-yond the pres-cribed periods.
